item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There needs to be a thing where each person has a page in their account thing where they can put links to all their favorite SCPs. Like this one. I hate it when I want to show someone an SCP but I can't remember what its called or where it is.
This was executed too realistically to be a joke. That's why I upvoted.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: Foundation Task Force members cosplaying as cowboys in assless chaps being necessary to help contain an anomaly? Aaaaaahahahahahaahaha, awesome. Take my upvote, TAKE IT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what the fuuuuuuuuck

also ty 2-years-ago-Scantron for pre-empting my question of is that woman's face photoshopped or not.

i upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to know more, but at the same time I really dont XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The look on her face is alone enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just gonna leave this here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why haven't I had this upvoted? One of the first article I read after finding out about this site; wonderfully strange.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After reading this SCP I have two questions left:

  1. Dafuq I just read?
  2. Was it a "write an article based on this picture" contest?

Didn't find any redeeming positive qualities in this one, so downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's the matter with the O5 redaction? It seems pretty random and unneeded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand why her family was jailed for a homicide investigation. They found her dead watching TV, and any half-dollar investigative officer could've made the determination on-site before transferring her for a medical examination to prove she didn't die of foul play.

Additionally, how would the Foundation know she was anomalous so quickly? Identifying her would be close to impossible so soon after the death. Just a couple of plot holes. I'll upvote in faith that they'll be fixed, since withheld votes are silly when one otherwise likes the material. +1 for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So there's actually a point to Burning Man? That's the least believable thing I've read on this whole site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does feel a little… verbose at times, I don't know why the sculpture can be about 10 sentences long and yet this is so big. I still like it though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's fucking brilliant! I love the idea of the task force that has to deal with this shit!
"Okay, boys, I've just come back from a shift in 076's underwater death fortress, what have you been up to?"
"Well boss, it's time for 1570 to manifest again, so today we're gonna gay it up!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for an image/caption combination that is enough to compel you to read the whole article. Am I right in assuming that the butt-baring fellas are the MTF agents? I don't know whether that's the best MTF you want to be assigned to, or the worst.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Marjory always liked to watch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, she's roughly 5'5'' 100 lb, but it indicates that there has been no loss of mass or density and she requires plus sized clothes. So she's obese but has less mass than she should. But she's no less dense.

On the other hand, trashy little (big) old lady ghost who is controlled by staging debaucheries. For some reason, the existence of such a thing amuses me to no end.

*most images are blocked where I'm viewing this, so I may be missing something

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're correct of course, but I believe that clinical supercedes political correctness in this format. Moot point given Sorts' point, but still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …huh. And here I assumed it was solely a P/C euphamism. Carry on then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In regards to clothing it actually is, I spent 12 years of my life working for a catalog company that dealt with it. It's the official nice way to say "clothes for fat people."

Edit: To clarify, it's a specific instruction about where to go to get her new clothes. They have to hit the plus-size stores and catalogs. Lane Bryant and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: subject is suffering from obesity. Clinical isn't very polite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like the new information has been added since last I checked. I like it.

As an aside, "plus-sized" isn't terribly clinical…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is pretty cool. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What in the hell. No, seriously, what in the hell?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That lady's face will be in my nightmares forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hahaha

dude what

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't want any of your cake. Not ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't care what HAS happened, or what WILL happen, with this SCP. I'm upvoting for that freaking woman's expression, the rest is just frosting on the cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DAT ASS
SHUN IT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nay good sir. Especially since chaps were specifically designed to be worn over jeans (something folks playing dress-up intentionally forget) to protect the clothing and legs from either dense terrain such as thickets, or incidental contact while herding cattle. It's believed that they originated from copying similar styles from Native American tribes.

Fun fact: this is also why bikers wear leather and denim, because they're a combination of comfortable clothes that will keep your skin from looking like hamburger if you wipe out. Unless you're one of those fine fellows that wears an open leather vest and no shirt or helmet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had to take a closer look at the picture to see if what I'm seeing is a weird shadow or bad Photoshop… Only to realize you have me staring at a picture of rowdy cowboys with their asses out. I feel a bit obligated to upvote just for that.
I'm going to keep my vote still, though, and see where this goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That picture … I'm sorry, I just can't …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I wasn't trying to say you where. I meant the idea being put to use by Sorts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback on tone, I'll work on that in the next draft, which should also make the displays into more of an effective containment measure.

The last bit about "protecting normalcy" is important though, since the theme is about how the Foundation is organizing a constant stream of gratuitous counterculture displays so that the harmless ghost of a weird old lady doesn't threaten normalcy. That's the whole incongruity that I'm hanging the piece on, which is why I stuck that at the end with a lolbutts note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Procedures into fully containing SCP-1570 are still being researched, although current procedures have been largely successful at preventing public exposure.

This is worded strangely. I would shift this sentence to after you actually explain how it's contained, and say something like:

Research into full containment of SCP-1570 is ongoing, though current procedures have been largely successful at controlling manifestations and preventing public knowledge.

You say this:

The projections always display any clothing and items currently in physical contact with SCP-1570, including personnel and the surface upon which SCP-1570 might be standing at a range of 4.62 meters out from itself.

then this:

Keeping physical contact to a minimum and magnetically levitating SCP-1570 from the floor of the chamber is critical to preventing the public from paying too much attention to SCP-1570's projections.

Something about that second sentence just doesn't feel right. I can't think how to articulate why it's off beyond it explains the obvious. Specifically that they're trying to prevent the public paying too much attention to it. They're the Foundation, that's what they do.

Then this too:

Capitalizing on this behavior led to current containment procedure which both controls the phenomena and makes it less likely to threaten the normalcy of public life.

Controlling something so it's less likely to pose a threat or be noticed is generally the idea of containment. I don't think reiterating that serves any purpose.

Another thing that occurs to me with no relevance to my initial comment is that if it can be dissipated by just poking it, why wouldn't we do so? Not doing so at the first opportune moment risks someone else doing it accidentally at a distinctly inopportune one.

I may have missed it, but I saw nothing which suggested it would only appear once in a given time frame, with some predictable interval between manifestations allowing for this containment strategy to actually be meaningful. Without something like that specified, it doesn't sound like we can actually prevent it showing up where we don't want it, only get it to show up where we do from time to time. Like, we arrange an event in San Fran. There's no indication it couldn't show up elsewhere the very next day. As such, why getting it to show up in these places constitutes containment is unclear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hmm, point… actually the biggest problem on reflection is that she's a life form, which is not how I originally conceived of her. That's probably going to change though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I downvoted because the concept was too contrived, but the image of the woman being kept in a Lagrange point between SF, Austin, Vegas and Tijuana made me laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The term "bio-stasis chamber" bugs me, since it implies that the Foundation has some high-tech means of putting living organisms into stasis. And anyways, she's already in stasis, so putting her into a stasis chamber would be redundant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I'm not rewriting it. i just gave Sorts an idea of where to take this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love short, flat-out weird articles, but this is just…ehh. I'm looking forward to whatever Drewbear came up with, though. I really want to like this, but it's just not doing it for me right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really…

Umm…

Wait.

What?

Hmm.

Oooooooooooh!

No, that's not it.

…Yeah.

item.title: Re: The fuck?
item.content:encoded: My sentiments exactly. This is…interesting, but I don't really like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? I always thought there were assed chaps that no one bought anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: chaps are inherently assless.

item.title: The fuck?
item.content:encoded: Seriously what the fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was hoping the guys in the assless chaps would be the SCP, but I was horribly and sadly mistaken…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you trying to tell me that the people sticking their butts out aren't SCPs? That they are normal people?? Oh, God no!

Hold me, Mommy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Drewbear gave me a fantastic idea about how to expand on this, so I'm going to work on it further. Thanks to all of you that liked it but I can totally get why people don't. Hopefully the extra depth will help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It is weird, and kind of silly, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GAHHHH
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
I think she sees my soul

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"And now, I've seen everything."

poofs out of existence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It WAS a poor excuse to use the picture, won't lie about that. The whole thing pretty much boils down to "There's something wrong in this picture and it's not the guys sticking their butts out."

But could you elaborate on what you felt was being over explained regarding containment or the reader being too dense? I'm not sure how to strike a balance between "scientifically" describing magic holograms and brevity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really worried about reading this after seeing the picture. This was definitely an interesting read. Upvoted at incident "Mexican Flying Brujah." Also… that picture. Words truly elude me, here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is certainly one of them. The article pauses to explain itself like the reader is too dense to make simple connections or doesn't understand why the Foundation contains things, there's a humorous note because lol, and it's pretty dull. There's also a reference to a date and "anomalous events" which occurred with zero elaboration or context. This whole thing feels like a poor excuse to use that picture. I'm mystified this is positively rated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Art Hags would've been a pretty good name for the MTF, too. That being said, fucking great article for being such a… weird idea?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should give classes on how to turn random bullshit into an interesting SCP idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if it weren't so well written i'd agree with you

upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've done a lot of dumb things but writing this on a dare is certainly one of them
